title: Cursor Pagination, Ordering, Filters, and Snapshot Behavior · Go Retrieval

objectives: Choose a deterministic immutable-enough order with unique tie-breaker and document updates that can move entries.

Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Pagination Accessibility
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go Pagination Accessibility: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 3109 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove total ordering, validated cursors, keyset navigation, change behavior, and accessible controls; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Expose next and previous links, result counts or bounded uncertainty, stable headings, focus behavior, and no infinite-scroll requirement.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Pagination Accessibility.
steps.why: Retrieving Feed Go Pagination Accessibility through the concrete stable bounded reader timeline query case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Pagination Accessibility, keep Feed Go Pagination Accessibility connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Expose next and previous links, result counts or bounded uncertainty, stable headings, focus behavior, and no infinite-scroll requirement.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Expose next and previous links, result counts or bounded uncertainty, stable headings, focus behavior, and no infinite-scroll requirement.
